prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a3f7e1b09c4d28f6","BT":2,"BW":4,"HF":0.38,"RF":0.27,"MA":1,"hour_entropy":2.18,"behavioral_consistency":0.31,"response_latency_variance":7.42,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.54,"error_recovery_pattern":0.072,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.71,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.33,"gas_price_precision":0.58,"rationale":"Mimics a casual low-entropy human who transacts in narrow time windows with sloppy gas settings and high inter-action jitter, sitting comfortably below all five HasciDB thresholds while matching the left tails of hour_entropy and tip_precision and the right tail of latency variance."}
